items.hook: It Chapter Two is a 2019 American supernatural horror film directed by Andy Muschietti from a screenplay by Gary Dauberman.
items.body: It is the sequel to It (2017) and the second of a two-part adaptation of the 1986 novel It by Stephen King. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: William Shakespeare's play, The Merchant of Venice, is entered on the Stationers' Register. By decree of Queen Elizabeth, the Stationers' Register licensed printed works, giving the Crown tight control over all published material.
items.body: William Shakespeare was an English playwright, poet and actor. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: First Crusade: Godfrey of Bouillon is elected the first Defender of the Holy Sepulchre of The Kingdom of Jerusalem.
items.body: The First Crusade (1096–1099) was the first of a series of religious wars, or Crusades, which were initiated, supported and at times directed by the Latin Church in the Middle Ages. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Roanoke Colony: A second group of English settlers arrives on Roanoke Island off North Carolina to re-establish the deserted colony.
items.body: The Roanoke Colony was the site of two attempts by Sir Walter Raleigh to found the first permanent English settlement in North America. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: The Acts of Union 1707 are agreed upon by commissioners from the Kingdom of England and the Kingdom of Scotland, which, when passed by each country's Parliament, leads to the creation of the Kingdom of Great Britain.
items.body: The Acts of Union refer to two acts of Parliament, one by the Parliament of Scotland in March 1707, followed shortly thereafter by an equivalent act of the Parliament of England. One of those things you can't un-know.
